member.name: Suella Braverman
member.fullTitle: Rt Hon Suella Braverman KC MP
member.synopsis: The Rt Hon Suella Braverman is the Reform UK MP for Fareham and Waterlooville , and has been an MP continually since 7 May 2015.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Pre-school Education: Apprentices
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what support her Department provides to nursery managers seeking to use Level 2 and Level 3 apprentices more flexibly to meet local childcare demand.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department offers a variety of qualification pathways to support individuals to gain the vital skills they need to work in an early years setting, including apprenticeships at levels 2, 3, 5 and 6. Apprentices play a vital role in our early years work...

writtenQuestions.heading: Pre-school Education: Apprentices
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, if her Department will conduct a review of apprenticeship deployment rules in nursery settings to ensure they reflect the operational needs of providers and support the expansion of childcare places.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department offers a variety of qualification pathways to support individuals to gain the vital skills they need to work in an early years setting, including apprenticeships at levels 2, 3, 5 and 6. Apprentices play a vital role in our early years work...

writtenQuestions.heading: Pre-school Education: Apprentices
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, if her Department will update guidance to allow nursery managers discretion in counting Level 2 and Level 3 apprentices towards staffing ratios where appropriate supervision is in place.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department offers a variety of qualification pathways to support individuals to gain the vital skills they need to work in an early years setting, including apprenticeships at levels 2, 3, 5 and 6. Apprentices play a vital role in our early years work...

writtenQuestions.heading: Pre-school Education: Apprentices
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what assessment her Department has made of the potential impact of Level 2 and Level 3 apprenticeship requirements on staffing flexibility in early years settings.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department offers a variety of qualification pathways to support individuals to gain the vital skills they need to work in an early years setting, including apprenticeships at levels 2, 3, 5 and 6. Additional flexibilities were introduced in 2024 to g...

writtenQuestions.heading: Pre-school Education: Apprentices
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps she is taking to address workforce shortages in early years settings arising from restrictions on the deployment of Level 2 and Level 3 apprentices.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Best Start in Life Strategy lays the foundation for long-term improvements to recruitment and retention in the early years sector. Initiatives to improve recruitment and retention include the ‘Do something BIG’ recruitment campaign, financial incentiv...

writtenQuestions.heading: Civil Servants: Political Impartiality
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what safeguards are in place to ensure that public sector employees are not subject to political vetting, pressure or monitoring by internal staff networks.
writtenQuestions.answerText: All civil servants must follow the Civil Service Code, including when acting in a volunteer civil service staff network capacity. This includes acting with political impartiality at all times. The public sector is not bound by the Civil Service Code but p...

writtenQuestions.heading: Antisemitism: Arts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what recent discussions she has had with the Charity Commission on antisemitism in the arts.
writtenQuestions.answerText: All forms of racial and religious discrimination are completely unacceptable and have no place in our arts charities. The Secretary of State recently met with the Charity Commission for England and Wales to discuss how to make sure it has the powers it ne...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Prostate Cancer: Screening
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what the timetable is for consideration of the final outcome of the UK National Screening Committee's evaluation of prostate cancer screening.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We have accepted the recommendation of the UK National Screening Committee (UK NSC) to introduce England's first targeted prostate cancer screening programme. The programme, rolling out in 2027, will screen men between 45 and 61 years old who have a BRCA2...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Children in Care: Richmond upon Thames
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether she has made an assessment of the adequacy of record-keeping by Richmond upon Thames Council relating to looked-after children in the 1970s and 1980s.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member for Fareham and Waterlooville to the answer of 2 June 2026 Question 2037.

writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he is taking to ensure that NHS staff who raise concerns about workplace privacy, dignity or safety are protected from victimisation or disciplinary action.
